annotations.label: Coprime integers

anchor.snippet: two integers a and b are coprime

annotations.note: `Nat.Coprime m n := Nat.gcd m n = 1` is the standard definition (with `IsCoprime` as the Bézout-style version in Mathlib.RingTheory.Coprime.Basic).

annotations.label: 8 and 9 are coprime

anchor.snippet: The numbers 8 and 9 are coprime

annotations.note: No specific lemma for the numeric instance gcd(8,9)=1 was found.

annotations.label: 6 and 9 are not coprime

anchor.snippet: 6 and 9 are not coprime

annotations.note: No specific lemma for the numeric non-instance of gcd(6,9)=3 was found.

annotations.label: Notation for coprimality

anchor.snippet: the standard way of expressing this fact in mathematical notation

annotations.note: The notational fact gcd(a,b)=1 ↔ Coprime is expressed via `Nat.coprime_iff_gcd_eq_one`, but Mathlib does not introduce the `(a,b)=1` notation itself.

annotations.label: Euclidean algorithm tests coprimality

anchor.snippet: A fast way to determine whether two numbers are coprime

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the Euclidean recursion (`Nat.gcd`) to compute gcd and thus test coprimality, but the methodological claim itself is not stated as a theorem.

annotations.label: Euler's totient function

anchor.snippet: The number of integers coprime with a positive integer

annotations.note: Defined as `#{a ∈ range n | n.Coprime a}` exactly matching Wikipedia's count.

annotations.label: Setwise and pairwise coprime

anchor.snippet: A set of integers can also be called coprime

annotations.note: Pairwise coprime is expressed via `Pairwise (IsCoprime on f)` / `Set.Pairwise s (IsCoprime · ·)`, but a named `Setwise.Coprime` predicate (gcd of the whole set = 1) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: {2,3,4} setwise but not pairwise coprime

anchor.snippet: The set {2, 3, 4} is coprime

annotations.note: No specific lemma instantiating this example was found.

annotations.label: 1 and -1 coprime with every integer

anchor.snippet: The numbers 1 and −1 are the only integers coprime with every integer

annotations.note: `Nat.coprime_one_left`/`Nat.coprime_one_right` (and `isCoprime_one_left`/`right`) cover the affirmative direction; the uniqueness characterization isn't stated as a single lemma.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for coprimality

anchor.snippet: A number of conditions are equivalent to a and b being coprime

annotations.note: Several equivalences exist individually (`isCoprime_iff_gcd_eq_one`, `isCoprime_iff_exists`, `Nat.coprime_iff_isRelPrime`), but they are not bundled into one `TFAE`-style statement.

annotations.label: Cancellation modulo a

anchor.snippet: As a consequence of the third point

annotations.note: Cancellation of a coprime factor in a congruence is captured by `Nat.ModEq.cancel_left_div_gcd`/`cancel_right_div_gcd` (which specializes to ordinary cancellation when the multiplier is coprime to the modulus).

annotations.label: Euclid's lemma

anchor.snippet: this also follows from the first point by Euclid's lemma

annotations.note: Formalized as `IsCoprime.dvd_of_dvd_mul_right` (and the Int version in `Mathlib.Data.Int.GCD` is explicitly labeled `Euclid's lemma`).

annotations.label: Coprimality of powers

anchor.snippet: As a consequence of the first point, if a and b are coprime, then so are any powers

annotations.note: `IsCoprime.pow`/`Nat.Coprime.pow` (with `_left`, `_right`, `_iff` variants) prove that powers preserve coprimality.

annotations.label: Generalization of Euclid's lemma

anchor.snippet: If a and b are coprime and a divides the product bc

annotations.note: This is the same statement as Euclid's lemma above and is proved as `IsCoprime.dvd_of_dvd_mul_right`/`_left`.

annotations.label: Visibility from origin characterization

anchor.snippet: would be "visible" via an unobstructed line of sight from the origin

annotations.note: No 'visibility from origin' lattice-point lemma was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Probability of coprimality is 6/pi^2

anchor.snippet: the probability that two randomly chosen integers are coprime is 6

annotations.note: The density/limit statement 6/π² for pairwise coprimality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coprimality of 2^a-1 and 2^b-1

anchor.snippet: Two natural numbers a and b are coprime if and only if the numbers

annotations.note: Grep for `2^a-1`/two_pow-minus-one coprimality did not turn up a corresponding lemma.

annotations.label: Setwise coprime set

anchor.snippet: can also be called coprime or setwise coprime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no named predicate for 'gcd of a whole set equals 1'; only the binary `IsCoprime`/`Nat.Coprime` and the pairwise version exist.

annotations.label: 6, 10, 15 setwise coprime

anchor.snippet: the integers 6, 10, 15 are coprime

annotations.note: No specific example instance was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pairwise coprime

anchor.snippet: If every pair in a set of integers is coprime

annotations.note: Expressed throughout Mathlib as `Pairwise (IsCoprime on f)` / `Set.Pairwise s fun i j => IsCoprime (g i) (g j)`.

annotations.label: 4, 5, 6 setwise but not pairwise coprime

anchor.snippet: the integers 4, 5, 6 are (setwise) coprime

annotations.note: No specific example instance was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinite pairwise coprime sets

anchor.snippet: It is possible for an infinite set of integers to be pairwise coprime

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma constructing an infinite pairwise-coprime family (Fermat numbers, primes, etc.) under that label was found.

annotations.label: Limit of probability P_N is 6/pi^2

anchor.snippet: one can show that in the limit

annotations.note: The asymptotic density theorem P_N → 6/π² is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Probability of k integers setwise coprime

anchor.snippet: the probability of k randomly chosen integers being setwise coprime

annotations.note: The 1/ζ(k) density of k-tuples that are setwise coprime is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ternary tree of coprime pairs

anchor.snippet: All pairs of positive coprime numbers

annotations.note: The Stern-Brocot/ternary-tree enumeration of coprime pairs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Calkin-Wilf tree generates coprime pairs

anchor.snippet: Another (much simpler) way to generate a tree of positive coprime pairs

annotations.note: Grep for `Calkin`/`Wilf`/`Stern_Brocot` in Mathlib found no matches.

annotations.label: Gear design application

anchor.snippet: In machine design, an even, uniform gear wear

annotations.note: Engineering application; not a mathematical statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cryptography application

anchor.snippet: In pre-computer cryptography

annotations.note: Historical/applied note; not a mathematical statement formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coprime polynomials

anchor.snippet: polynomials whose greatest common divisor is 1 are called coprime polynomials

annotations.note: `IsCoprime p q` applied to polynomials over a field gives coprime polynomials (used extensively in e.g. `Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.PartialFractions`).

annotations.label: Coprime ideals

anchor.snippet: Two ideals A and B in a commutative ring R are called coprime

annotations.note: `Ideal.isCoprime_iff_add : IsCoprime I J ↔ I + J = 1` captures the standard definition of coprime ideals.

annotations.label: Generalized Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.snippet: The Chinese remainder theorem can be generalized to any commutative ring

annotations.note: `Ideal.quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient` is the generalized CRT for finitely many pairwise coprime ideals in a commutative ring.
